title: Guajava basantha
intro: I couldn't find any information on Guajava basantha. However, I found information on Guajava, also known as Psidium guajava, which is a tropical tree that belongs to the Myrtaceae family. Guajava is a deciduous tree known for its edible fruit and is commonly used in traditional medicine due to its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to help with wound healing, digestive issues, and other health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Guajava include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and ascorbic acid, and it is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being used fresh or dried in various traditional remedies.
